generatedSentence: Captain Jax walks back from a vintage pizza place, his googles reflecting a wisp of wind that signals a coming wormhole. He'd lucked into a random date with a notorious juggler from a high class society, a chimera of a person, whose powerful digestion of polyp-shaped snacks always made him count his blessings. The geography of the city was changing, an erasure he felt in his kneecap, and the distant sound of hummers made him wonder about the jackrabbit pace of his life. Suddenly, a herald of the oligarchy, a bloated figure known for sacked cities, transports a trio of triplets who had awoken from deep space, their lingual curses echoing. Their offensive tactics, an overhand volley of oblong flapjack bombs, blocked the main street into gridlock. It was pure folly to challenge them, a vagrant group from the Artics with unreadable phantasies of power. This multicultural force threatened the townhall, but Jax wins, determined to flatten their seemingly flawless advance. Jax, the daredevil, now a joyful artist, felt the mysterious glowing organ in his chest thrumming like a gearbox. He remembered the negative image of a cauldron from offworld, a prickly place where nepotism was king. With an unfettered idea, he used a signal to summon spectral elephants, their sheer prosperity a talisman against the enemy. He knew the fight would be a handover, but he refused to let hope fall, despite the strain on his tendons in the pavilion of wheat. He found the vulnerable cipher in their plan, a hidden estuary of English blame that caused them to vomit. With an oximeter reading his lucky breath, he watched the dimmer lights of their backstage base. Jax, the artist, felt an inflow of equity and could appreciate the month-long journey. A wallaby and parrot, his companions, helped locate the joiner, a wright who could help forgive them. His sartorial elegance wasn't sacked, but he knew he'd need tickets to fix the future.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A cyber-enhanced Captain Jax, wearing a silver chrome helmet with glowing red visor and a high-tech armored jumpsuit. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Captain Jax walks away from a vintage pizza place, looking at his glowing wrist-mounted googles, with a subtle distortion in the background hinting at a wormhole, eye-level shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A bloated herald of the oligarchy stands before three identical triplets, who are blocking a neon-lit street with oblong flapjack-like bombs, low-angle shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Captain Jax, now a daredevil, directs massive, glowing spectral elephants against the oligarchy's pavilion made of wheat, an epic wide shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Captain Jax, a wallaby, and a parrot locate a hidden joiner in a dimly lit backstage room, Jax holds an oximeter, medium shot.

definitions.definition: To move on the feet by alternately setting each foot (or pair or group of feet, in the case of animals with four or more feet) forward, with at least one foot on the ground at all times. Compare run.
definitions.example: To walk briskly for an hour every day is to keep fit.

definitions.definition: To "walk free", i.e. to win, or avoid, a criminal court case, particularly when actually guilty.
definitions.example: If you can’t present a better case, that robber is going to walk.

definitions.definition: Of an object, to go missing or be stolen.
definitions.example: If you leave your wallet lying around, it’s going to walk.

definitions.definition: (of a batsman) To walk off the field, as if given out, after the fielding side appeals and before the umpire has ruled; done as a matter of sportsmanship when the batsman believes he is out.
To travel (a distance) by walking.
definitions.example: I walk two miles to school every day.  The museum’s not far from here – you can walk it.

definitions.definition: To take for a walk or accompany on a walk.
definitions.example: I walk the dog every morning.  Will you walk me home?

definitions.definition: To allow a batter to reach base by pitching four balls.
To move something by shifting between two positions, as if it were walking.
definitions.example: I carefully walked the ladder along the wall.

definitions.definition: To full; to beat cloth to give it the consistency of felt.
To traverse by walking (or analogous gradual movement).
definitions.example: I walked the streets aimlessly.   Debugging this computer program involved walking the heap.

definitions.definition: To operate the left and right throttles of (an aircraft) in alternation.
To leave, resign.
definitions.example: If we don't offer him more money he'll walk.

definitions.definition: To push (a vehicle) alongside oneself as one walks.
To behave; to pursue a course of life; to conduct oneself.
To be stirring; to be abroad; to go restlessly about; said of things or persons expected to remain quiet, such as a sleeping person, or the spirit of a dead person.
To be in motion; to act; to move.
To put, keep, or train (a puppy) in a walk, or training area for dogfighting.
(hotel) To move a guest to another hotel if their confirmed reservation is not available on day of check-in.
A trip made by walking.
definitions.example: I take a walk every morning

definitions.definition: A distance walked.
definitions.example: It’s a long walk from my house to the library

definitions.definition: An Olympic Games track event requiring that the heel of the leading foot touch the ground before the toe of the trailing foot leaves the ground.
A manner of walking; a person's style of walking.
definitions.example: The Ministry of Silly Walks is underfunded this year

definitions.definition: A path, sidewalk/pavement or other maintained place on which to walk. Compare trail.
A situation where all players fold to the big blind, as their first action (instead of calling or raising), once they get their cards.
An award of first base to a batter following four balls being thrown by the pitcher; known in the rules as a "base on balls".
definitions.example: The pitcher now has two walks in this inning alone

definitions.definition: In coffee, coconut, and other plantations, the space between them.
(Belize) An area of an estate planted with fruit-bearing trees.
A place for keeping and training puppies for dogfighting.
An enclosed area in which a gamecock is confined to prepare him for fighting.
A sequence of alternating vertices and edges, where each edge's endpoints are the preceding and following vertices in the sequence.
Something very easily accomplished; a walk in the park.
A cheque drawn on a bank that was not a member of the London Clearing and whose sort code was allocated on a one-off basis; they had to be "walked" (hand-delivered by messengers).
A hole burrowed by a worm
A hypothetical shortcut between two points in spacetime, permitting faster-than-light travel and sometimes time travel.
A location in a monitor program containing the address of a routine, allowing the user to substitute different functionality.
To make porous or permeable through the formation of small holes or tunnels.
A small bundle, as of straw or other like substance; any slender, flexible structure or group.
definitions.example: A wisp of hair escaped her barrette and whipped wildly in the wind.

definitions.definition: A whisk, or small broom.
A will o' the wisp, or ignis fatuus.
An immeasurable, indefinable essence of life; soul.
A disease affecting the feet of cattle.
To brush or dress, as with a wisp.
To rumple.
To produce a wisp, as of smoke.
Real or perceived movement of atmospheric air usually caused by convection or differences in air pressure.
definitions.example: As they accelerated onto the motorway, the wind tore the plywood off the car's roof-rack.

definitions.definition: Air artificially put in motion by any force or action.
definitions.example: the wind of a cannon ball;  the wind of a bellows

definitions.definition: The ability to breathe easily.
definitions.example: After the second lap he was already out of wind.

definitions.definition: News of an event, especially by hearsay or gossip. (Used with catch, often in the past tense.)
definitions.example: Steve caught wind of Martha's dalliance with his best friend.

definitions.definition: One of the five basic elements in Indian and Japanese models of the Classical elements).
Flatus.
definitions.example: Eww. Someone just passed wind.

definitions.definition: Breath modulated by the respiratory and vocal organs, or by an instrument.
The woodwind section of an orchestra. Occasionally also used to include the brass section.
A direction from which the wind may blow; a point of the compass; especially, one of the cardinal points, which are often called the "four winds".
Types of playing-tile in the game of mah-jongg, named after the four winds.
A disease of sheep, in which the intestines are distended with air, or rather affected with a violent inflammation. It occurs immediately after shearing.
Mere breath or talk; empty effort; idle words.
A bird, the dotterel.
The region of the solar plexus, where a blow may paralyze the diaphragm and cause temporary loss of breath or other injury.
To blow air through a wind instrument or horn to make a sound.
To cause (someone) to become breathless, as by a blow to the abdomen, or by physical exertion, running, etc.
definitions.example: The boxer was winded during round two.

definitions.definition: To cause a baby to bring up wind by patting its back after being fed.
To turn a boat or ship around, so that the wind strikes it on the opposite side.
To expose to the wind; to winnow; to ventilate.
To perceive or follow by scent.
definitions.example: The hounds winded the game.

definitions.definition: To rest (a horse, etc.) in order to allow the breath to be recovered; to breathe.
To turn a windmill so that its sails face into the wind.
An attack.
definitions.example: The Marines today launched a major offensive.

definitions.definition: The posture of attacking or being able to attack.
definitions.example: He took the offensive in the press, accusing his opponent of corruption.

definitions.definition: Causing offense; arousing a visceral reaction of disgust, anger, or hatred.
definitions.example: An offensive smell.

definitions.definition: Relating to an offense or attack, as opposed to defensive.
definitions.example: The army's offensive capabilities. An offensive weapon.

definitions.definition: Having to do with play directed at scoring.
definitions.example: The offensive coordinator is responsible for ordering all rushing plays.

definitions.definition: A group, collection, category or set sharing characteristics or attributes.
definitions.example: Often used to imply membership of a large class.

definitions.definition: A social grouping, based on job, wealth, etc. In Britain, society is commonly split into three main classes; upper class, middle class and working class.
The division of society into classes.
definitions.example: Jane Austen's works deal with class in 18th-century England.

definitions.definition: Admirable behavior; elegance.
definitions.example: Apologizing for losing your temper, even though you were badly provoked, showed real class.

definitions.definition: A group of students in a regularly scheduled meeting with a teacher.
definitions.example: The class was noisy, but the teacher was able to get their attention with a story.

definitions.definition: A series of lessons covering a single subject.
definitions.example: I took the cooking class for enjoyment, but I also learned a lot.

definitions.definition: A group of students who commenced or completed their education during a particular year. A school class.
definitions.example: The class of 1982 was particularly noteworthy.

definitions.definition: A category of seats in an airplane, train or other means of mass transportation.
definitions.example: I used to fly business class, but now my company can only afford economy.

definitions.definition: A rank in the classification of organisms, below phylum and above order; a taxon of that rank.
definitions.example: Magnolias belong to the class Magnoliopsida.

definitions.definition: Best of its kind.
definitions.example: It is the class of Italian bottled waters.

definitions.definition: A grouping of data values in an interval, often used for computation of a frequency distribution.
A collection of sets definable by a shared property.
definitions.example: Every set is a class, but classes are not generally sets. A class that is not a set is called a proper class.

definitions.definition: A group of people subject to be conscripted in the same military draft, or more narrowly those persons actually conscripted in a particular draft.
A set of objects having the same behavior (but typically differing in state), or a template defining such a set.
definitions.example: an abstract base class

definitions.definition: One of the sections into which a Methodist church or congregation is divided, supervised by a class leader.
To assign to a class; to classify.
definitions.example: I would class this with most of the other mediocre works of the period.

definitions.definition: To be grouped or classed.
To divide into classes, as students; to form into, or place in, a class or classes.
Great; fabulous
Agent noun of juggle; one who either literally juggles objects, or figuratively juggles tasks.
A person who practices juggling.
A person who performs tricks using sleight of hand, a conjurer, prestidigitator.
A magician or wizard.
A baked Italian dish of a thinly rolled bread dough crust typically topped before baking with tomato sauce, cheese and other ingredients such as meat, vegetables or fruit
definitions.example: Want to go out for pizza tonight?

definitions.definition: A single instance of this dish
definitions.example: He ate a whole pizza!

definitions.definition: An abnormal growth protruding from a mucous membrane
A cylindrical coelenterate, such as the hydra, having a mouth surrounded with tentacles
Having, or capable of exerting power, potency or influence.
Large; capacious; said of veins of ore.
To a great extent or degree.
definitions.example: Not very many (of them) had been damaged.

definitions.definition: Conforming to fact, reality or rule; true.
(with superlatives) Used to firmly establish that nothing else surpasses in some respect.
definitions.example: He was the very best runner there.

definitions.definition: One who hums.
A Humvee.
A type of vehicle resembling a jeep but bulkier.
definitions.example: The newlyweds took a hummer limo back to their casino resort.

definitions.definition: A hummingbird.
A humdinger.
A fastball.
Fellatio in which the person performing the act vibrates their mouth by humming.
A very energetic or lively person, a powerful lively thing.
Something or someone exceptional of their type.
An obvious lie.
A liar.
An erection of the penis.
An arrest on false pretexts.
The process, in the gastrointestinal tract, by which food is converted into substances that can be utilized by the body.
The result of this process.
The ability to use this process.
The processing of decay in organic matter assisted by microorganisms.
The assimilation and understanding of ideas.
Generation of pus; suppuration.
Dissolution of a sample into a solution by means of adding acid and heat.
To deliver googlies.
To move as a ball in a googly.
An Internet search, such as those performed on the Google search engine.
A match obtained by a query in the Google search engine.
definitions.example: The word "oceanfront" has 6,150,000 googles, so I think it must be a real word.

definitions.definition: To search for (something) on the Internet using the Google search engine.
definitions.example: Tom googles all of his prospective girlfriends.

definitions.definition: (by extension) To search for (something) on the Internet using any comprehensive search engine.
definitions.example: I googled him but there were no references to him on the Internet.

definitions.definition: To be locatable in a search of the Internet.
definitions.example: His name googles.

definitions.definition: The flat, roundish bone in the knee.
(roofing) A metal cover trim that fits over a panel rib after it has been cut and bent.
A cap or strong covering for the knees, used chiefly for horses, to protect their knees in case of a fall.
To destroy the knees of (a person), usually by shooting at the knees, as a punishment carried out by criminals or terrorists.
Widely known, especially for something negative; infamous.
Alternative letter-case form of Chimera (a flame-spewing monster often represented as having two heads, one of a goat and the other of a lion; the body of a goat; and a serpent as a tail).
Any fantastic creature with parts from different animals.
Anything composed of very disparate parts.
definitions.example: The car which he built himself was a real chimera: half Volkswagen and half Porsche.

definitions.definition: A foolish, incongruous, or vain thought or product of the imagination.
A grotesque like a gargoyle, but without a spout for rainwater.
An organism with genetically distinct cells originating from two or more zygotes.
Usually chimaera: a cartilaginous marine fish in the subclass Holocephali and especially the order Chimaeriformes, with a blunt snout, long tail, and a spine before the first dorsal fin.
A chief or leader.
The person lawfully in command of a ship or other vessel.
definitions.example: The captain is the last man to leave a sinking ship.

definitions.definition: An army officer with a rank between the most senior grade of lieutenant and major.
A naval officer with a rank between commander and commodore.

A commissioned officer in the United States Navy, Coast Guard, NOAA Corps, or PHS Corps of a grade superior to a commander and junior to a rear admiral (lower half). A captain is equal in grade or rank to an Army, Marine Corps, or Air Force colonel.
One of the athletes on a sports team who is designated to make decisions, and is allowed to speak for his team with a referee or official.
The leader of a group of workers.
definitions.example: John Henry said to the captain, "A man ain't nothing but a man."

definitions.definition: The head boy of a school.
A maître d', a headwaiter.
An honorific title given to a prominent person. See colonel.
To act as captain
To exercise command of a ship, aircraft or sports team.
The act of counting or tallying a quantity.
definitions.example: Give the chairs a quick count to check if we have enough.

definitions.definition: The result of a tally that reveals the number of items in a set; a quantity counted.
A countdown.
A charge of misconduct brought in a legal proceeding.
The number of balls and strikes, respectively, on a batter's in-progress plate appearance.
definitions.example: He has a 3–2 count with the bases loaded.

definitions.definition: An object of interest or account; value; estimation.
To recite numbers in sequence.
definitions.example: Can you count to a hundred?

definitions.definition: To determine the number (of objects in a group).
definitions.example: There are three apples; count them.

definitions.definition: To be of significance; to matter.
definitions.example: Your views don’t count here.    It does count if you cheat with someone when you’re drunk.

definitions.definition: To be an example of something: often followed by as and an indefinite noun.
definitions.example: Apples count as a type of fruit.

definitions.definition: To consider something an example of something.
definitions.example: He counts himself a hero after saving the cat from the river.   I count you as more than a friend.

definitions.definition: To take account or note (of).
To plead orally; to argue a matter in court; to recite a count.
(grammar) Countable.
The action of erasing; deletion; obliteration.
The state of having been erased; total blankness.
The place where something has been erased.
definitions.example: There were several erasures on the paper.

definitions.definition: A tendency to ignore or conceal an element of society.
definitions.example: bisexual erasure

definitions.definition: The fruit of the date palm, Phoenix dactylifera, somewhat in the shape of an olive, containing a soft, sweet pulp and enclosing a hard kernel.
definitions.example: We made a nice cake from dates.

definitions.definition: The date palm.
definitions.example: There were a few dates planted around the house.

definitions.definition: To succeed by chance.
definitions.example: His plan lucked out.

definitions.definition: To rely on luck.
definitions.example: No plan. We're just to going to have to luck through.

definitions.definition: To carry out relying on luck.
definitions.example: Our plan is to luck it through.

definitions.definition: A roving motion; course without definite direction; lack of rule or method; chance.
Speed, full speed; impetuosity, force.
The full range of a bullet or other projectile; hence, the angle at which a weapon is tilted to allow the greatest range.
An undefined, unknown or unimportant person; a person of no consequence.
definitions.example: The party was boring. It was full of randoms.

definitions.definition: The direction of a rake-vein.
Having unpredictable outcomes and, in the ideal case, all outcomes equally probable; resulting from such selection; lacking statistical correlation.
definitions.example: The flip of a fair coin is purely random.

definitions.definition: Of or relating to probability distribution.
definitions.example: A toss of loaded dice is still random, though biased.

definitions.definition: Pseudorandom; mimicking the result of random selection.
(somewhat colloquial) Representative and undistinguished; typical and average; selected for no particular reason.
definitions.example: A random American off the street couldn't tell the difference.

definitions.definition: (somewhat colloquial) Apropos of nothing; lacking context; unexpected; having apparent lack of plan, cause or reason.
definitions.example: That was a completely random comment.

definitions.definition: Characterized by or often saying random things; habitually using non sequiturs.
definitions.example: You're so random!

definitions.definition: A description of the earth: a treatise or textbook on geography; an atlas or gazetteer.
The study of the physical properties of the earth, including how humans affect and are affected by them.
Terrain: the physical properties of a region of the earth.
Any subject considered in terms of its physical distribution.
Similar books, studies, or regions concerning other planets.
The physical arrangement of any place, particularly a house.
The lavatory: a room used for urination and defecation.
The relative arrangement of the parts of anything.
A territory: a geographical area as a field of business or market sector.
The rear of the body, especially the part between the neck and the end of the spine and opposite the chest and belly.
definitions.example: Could you please scratch my back?

definitions.definition: That which is farthest away from the front.
definitions.example: He sat in the back of the room.

definitions.definition: Upper part of a natural object which is considered to resemble an animal's back.
definitions.example: The small boat raced over the backs of the waves.

definitions.definition: A support or resource in reserve.
The keel and keelson of a ship.
definitions.example: The ship's back broke in the pounding surf.

definitions.definition: The roof of a horizontal underground passage.
Effort, usually physical.
definitions.example: Put some back into it!

definitions.definition: A non-alcoholic drink (often water or a soft drink), to go with hard liquor or a cocktail.
definitions.example: Could I get a martini with a water back?

definitions.definition: Among leather dealers, one of the thickest and stoutest tanned hides.
To go in the reverse direction.
definitions.example: the train backed into the station;  the horse refuses to back

definitions.definition: To support.
definitions.example: I back you all the way;  which horse are you backing in this race?

definitions.definition: (of the wind) To change direction contrary to the normal pattern; that is, to shift anticlockwise in the northern hemisphere, or clockwise in the southern hemisphere.
(of a square sail) To brace the yards so that the wind presses on the front of the sail, to slow the ship.
(of an anchor) To lay out a second, smaller anchor to provide additional holding power.
(of a hunting dog) To stand still behind another dog which has pointed.
To push or force backwards.
definitions.example: The mugger backed her into a corner and demanded her wallet.

definitions.definition: To get upon the back of; to mount.
To place or seat upon the back.
To make a back for; to furnish with a back.
definitions.example: to back books

definitions.definition: To adjoin behind; to be at the back of.
To write upon the back of, possibly as an endorsement.
definitions.example: to back a letter;  to back a note or legal document

definitions.definition: (of a justice of the peace) To sign or endorse (a warrant, issued in another county, to apprehend an offender).
To row backward with (oars).
definitions.example: to back the oars

definitions.definition: Near the rear.
definitions.example: Go in the back door of the house.

definitions.definition: Not current.
definitions.example: I’d like to find a back issue of that magazine.

definitions.definition: Far from the main area.
definitions.example: They took a back road.

definitions.definition: In arrear; overdue.
definitions.example: They still owe three months' back rent.

definitions.definition: Moving or operating backward.
definitions.example: back action

definitions.definition: Pronounced with the highest part of the body of the tongue toward the back of the mouth, near the soft palate (most often describing a vowel).
definitions.example: The vowel of lot has a back vowel in most dialects of England.

definitions.definition: To or in a previous condition or place.
definitions.example: He gave back the money.   He needs his money back.   He was on vacation, but now he’s back.   The office fell into chaos when you left, but now order is back.

definitions.definition: Away from the front or from an edge.
definitions.example: Sit all the way back in your chair.

definitions.definition: In a manner that impedes.
definitions.example: Fear held him back.

definitions.definition: In a reciprocal manner; in return.
definitions.example: If you hurt me, I'll hurt you back.

definitions.definition: Earlier, ago.
definitions.example: I last saw him a day or two back.

definitions.definition: The yield of grapes or wine from a vineyard or district during one season.
Wine, especially high-quality, identified as to year and vineyard or district of origin.
The harvesting of a grape crop and the initial pressing of juice for winemaking.
The year or place in which something is produced.
To harvest (grapes).
To make (wine) from grapes.
Of or relating to a vintage, or to wine identified by a specific vintage.
Having an enduring appeal; high-quality.
Classic (such as watches, video or computer games from the 1980s and early 1990s, old magazines, etc.).
Any of several large North American hares of the genus Lepus
To make a sudden, rapid movement
Relating or pertaining to several different cultures.
definitions.example: 31.X.2001 Viewed from the boardrooms of Britain, the market is becoming more multicultural than could have been imagined just five years ago. - The Guardian (2001)

definitions.definition: An articulated lorry.
An articulated bus.
Foolishness.
definitions.example: It'd be folly.

definitions.definition: Thoughtless action resulting in tragic consequence.
A fanciful building built for purely ornamental reasons.
A group of three.
One of a group of three.
One of a group of three siblings born at the same time to the same mother.
A group of three notes played or written where two notes would ordinarily be; a form of tuplet.
A triquark.
A quantum state with three allowed projections for the spin.
A constructed gem consisting of a thin slice of natural opal sandwiched between a clear glass or quartz top and a synthetic or stone backing that enhances the colour of the opal.
The act of ejecting.
That which is ejected.
A sound articulated with the tongue
Related to the tongue
Related to language or linguistics
Articulated with the tongue
Of a tooth, on the side facing the tongue
Any of several coarse ferns, of the genus Pteridium, that form dense thickets; often poisonous to livestock.
An area of countryside heavily populated by this fern.
To fill (something) so that it is not possible to pass.

definitions.definition: To prevent (something or someone) from passing.
definitions.example: You're blocking the road – I can't get through!

definitions.definition: To prevent (something from happening or someone from doing something).

definitions.definition: To impede an opponent.

definitions.definition: To specify the positions and movements of the actors.

definitions.definition: To hit with a block.
To play a block shot.

definitions.definition: To wait.

definitions.definition: To stretch or mould (a knitted item, a hat, etc.) into the desired shape.
definitions.example: I blocked the mittens by wetting them and pinning them to a shaped piece of cardboard.

definitions.definition: To shape or sketch out roughly.
Obstructed impeding general movement.
definitions.example: This road is blocked, so we must find another route.

definitions.definition: Obstructed impeding total flow in a pipe, etc.
definitions.example: Call a plumber to unclog the blocked drain.

definitions.definition: Drunk
A person who wanders from place to place; a nomad, a wanderer.
(specifically) A person without settled employment or habitation who supports himself or herself by begging or some dishonest means; a tramp, a vagabond.
definitions.example: Every morning before work, I see that poor vagrant around the neighbourhood begging for food.

definitions.definition: Vagrans egista, a widely distributed Asian butterfly of the family Nymphalidae.
An animal, typically a bird, found outside its species' usual range.
To become conscious after having slept.
To cause (somebody) to stop sleeping.
To excite or to stir up something latent.
To rouse from a state of inaction or dormancy.
To come out of a state of inaction or dormancy.

definitions.definition: To put in a sack or sacks.

To plunder or pillage, especially after capture; to obtain spoils of war from.

definitions.definition: To tackle the quarterback behind the line of scrimmage, especially before he is able to throw a pass.
To discharge from a job or position; to fire.

definitions.definition: In the phrase sack out, to fall asleep. See also hit the sack.

definitions.definition: To conquer, defeat.
To reach some destination or object, despite difficulty or toil (now usually intransitive, with preposition or locative adverb).
To triumph or achieve victory in (a game, a war, etc.).
To gain (a prize) by succeeding in competition or contest.

To achieve victory.

definitions.definition: To have power, coercion or control.

definitions.definition: To obtain (something desired).

definitions.definition: To cause a victory for someone.

An individual victory.

Gain; profit; income.
Wealth; goods owned.

definitions.definition: A messenger, especially one bringing important news.

definitions.definition: A harbinger, giving signs of things to come.

To proclaim or announce an event.

definitions.definition: (usually passive) To greet something with excitement; to hail.

definitions.definition: A condition of total, interlocking traffic congestion on the streets or highways of a crowded city, in which no one can move because everyone is in someone else's way.

On a smaller scale: the situation in which cars enter a signal-controlled intersection too late during the green light cycle, and are unable to clear the intersection (due to congestion in the next block) when the light turns red, thus blocking the cross traffic when it's their turn to go. Repeated at enough intersections, this phenomenon can lead to citywide gridlock.
(by extension) any paralysis of a complex system due to severe congestion, conflict, or deadlock.
To cause traffic congestion
To cause to become distended.
(veterinary medicine) to get an overdistended rumen, talking of a ruminant.
To fill soft substance with gas, water, etc.; to cause to swell.
To become distended; to swell up.
To fill with vanity or conceit.
To preserve by slightly salting and lightly smoking.

definitions.definition: Swollen with fluid or gas.
Excessively or extremely large or wealthy.
(of software) Excessively overloaded with features, known as bloatware.

That which comes from one's imagination.
The literary genre generally dealing with themes of magic and the supernatural, imaginary worlds and creatures, etc.
A fantastical design.
The drug gamma-hydroxybutyric acid.
The innate, mental image of an object; the link between instinct and reality.

definitions.example: It's okay to get vulnerable every now and again.

definitions.definition: More likely to be exposed to malicious programs or viruses.
definitions.example: a vulnerable PC with no antivirus software

definitions.definition: A numeric character.
Any text character.
A combination or interweaving of letters, as the initials of a name; a device; a monogram.
definitions.example: a painter's cipher, an engraver's cipher, etc.

definitions.definition: A method of transforming a text in order to conceal its meaning.
definitions.example: The message was written in a simple cipher. Anyone could figure it out.

definitions.definition: A cryptographic system using an algorithm that converts letters or sequences of bits into ciphertext.
Ciphertext; a message concealed via a cipher.
definitions.example: The message is clearly a cipher, but I can't figure it out.

definitions.definition: A grouping of three digits in a number, especially when delimited by commas or periods:
definitions.example: The probability is 1 in 1,000,000,000,000,000 — a number having five ciphers of zeros.

definitions.definition: A fault in an organ valve which causes a pipe to sound continuously without the key having been pressed.
A hip-hop jam session.
The path (usually circular) shared cannabis takes through a group, an occasion of cannabis smoking.
Someone or something of no importance.
Zero.
To calculate.
definitions.example: I never learned much more than how to read and cipher.

definitions.definition: To write in code or cipher.
Of an organ pipe: to sound independent of the organ.
To decipher.
A maker of wooden furniture or fittings.
A woodworking machine used to prepare edges of wooden elements to join to other wood pieces.
